Government Expert in Charge of Hes


sian Fly Investigation.


There is a disastrous outbreak OF
Hessian fly now in progress in eS-
IRENE western Kentucky and Tennes-
see. In North Dakota a similar DIS
astrous attack is being overcome by
a minute parasite which was found
in that state last year, BY arunclal
rearjngs, carried on during the past
winter. we have been able to deter
mine the presence of millions or
these parasites in the wheat stubble
of last year, and we also know that
they have not yet left their winter
quarters. Half a ton of this infested
stubble has been dispatched from
North Dakota to the ravaged district
of Kentucky and Tennessee with the
hone that these minute parasites VIII
become established there this spring
and attack the young of the Hessian
fv. Bv this means the department
hopes to be able TO reduce the pest
and the probabilities of further dis
astrous ravages next fall in the
stricken territory, which will offer
further relief another year


The United States department Of
agriculture is al present making
strenuous efforts to aid the American
wheat grower in a practical manner
in reducing the annual losses due to
attacks of the Hessian fly.


IT has long been known that mod
erately late sowing in the fall is a
practical preventive measure. But
how late IT is necessary TO SOW in
autumn to bring about the desired
results has yet to be determined. We
know that this date varies greatly
with latitude and meteorological con-
